record.field: Audiobookshelf is a self-hosted server for managing and streaming your audiobooks and podcasts across every device.

Audiobookshelf is a self-hosted server for managing and streaming your audiobooks and podcasts across every device.

It organizes large audio libraries, fetches metadata and cover art automatically, and keeps listening progress in sync between the web player and official mobile apps. Designed as an open-source alternative to commercial services like Audible, it supports multiple users with independent progress and granular permissions, plus a built-in podcast subscriber that auto-downloads new episodes.

Key features
  • Separate Book and Podcast libraries with automatic metadata and cover lookup (Audnexus, Google, MusicBrainz)
  • Cross-device progress sync, bookmarks and sleep timer via official open-source iOS and Android apps plus a PWA web player
  • Multi-user support with per-user progress and custom access permissions
  • Built-in podcast downloader with feed subscriptions and episode retention rules
  • Broad format support (MP3, M4B, M4A, FLAC, OGG, OPUS) plus in-app ebook reading (EPUB, PDF, MOBI, CBZ/CBR)
  • Chromecast support, open RSS feeds and a documented REST API for third-party tools
Best for

Individuals, families and small communities who want to own and stream a private audiobook and podcast collection from their own server, with mobile playback and offline listening.

services: audiobookshelf: image: ghcr.io/advplyr/audiobookshelf:2.19.4 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' environment: - TZ=UTC volumes: - ./config:/config - ./metadata:/metadata - {{ audiobooks_path }}:/audiobooks container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
